steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb of the first clause?

hints.Caelum: 'Caelum' is a noun meaning 'heaven/sky' — it is the subject
hints.terra: 'Terra' is a noun meaning 'earth' — it is the second subject

steps.target: to pass away / to pass over
choices: to pass away / to pass over

hints.to cross: 'To cross' is 'transire' as well, but the primary meaning here is 'to pass away'
hints.to go: 'To go' is 'ire' — 'transire' is the compound trans- + ire

steps.reveal: transibunt = will pass away (3rd pl. future)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'transeunt' — 'transibunt' with -ibu- is future
hints.future perfect: Future perfect would be 'transierint' — 'transibunt' is simple future
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'transeant' — 'transibunt' is future indicative

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'eo' (to go) — 'transire' is the compound trans- + ire
hints.transfero, transferre, transtuli, transl: These are the parts of 'transfero' (to transfer/carry across)
hints.trado, tradere, tradidi, traditum: These are the parts of 'trado' (to hand over/betray)

hints.accusative: 'Caelum' looks the same in accusative, but here it is the subject — nominative
hints.vocative: Vocative would be used for direct address — this is a declarative statement
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'caelo' — 'Caelum' is nominative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'caeli' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'caeli' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'caeli' ends in -i

hints.ablative: Ablative 'terra' looks the same, but here it is joined by 'et' as a co-subject — nominative
hints.vocative: Vocative would be used for address — 'terra' is the subject here

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'terrae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'terrae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'terrae' ends in -ae

steps.reveal: verba = words (nom. pl., subject of second clause)

hints.accusative plural: 'Verba' is the same form in acc. pl., but it is the subject of 'non transibunt'
hints.ablative singular: Ablative singular would be 'verbo' — 'verba' is nominative/accusative plural
hints.genitive singular: Genitive singular would be 'verbi' — 'verba' ends in -a (neut. pl.)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'verbi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'verbi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'verbi' ends in -i

steps.prompt: What rhetorical structure does this sentence use?
steps.target: antithesis (contrasting heaven/earth passing vs. words enduring)
choices: antithesis (contrasting heaven/earth passing vs. words enduring)

hints.chiasmus: Chiasmus reverses word order (ABBA) — this is a contrast between two parallel clauses
hints.tricolon: Tricolon has three parallel elements — this has two contrasting clauses
hints.anaphora: Anaphora repeats an opening word — the repeated 'transibunt' is at the end, not the beginning

translation: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away
